headline: South Florida's tech scene is lowkey heating up fr — Palantir's massive Miami move just shifted everything

stories.title: Palantir Moves HQ to Miami, Becomes Largest Public Company in South Florida
stories.summary: The $300B AI giant just relocated from Denver to Miami, making it the biggest publicly traded company in South Florida. This is literally a watershed moment for the region's tech credibility.
stories.takeaway: Florida's not just a tax haven anymore — it's becoming serious infrastructure for national security and AI.

stories.title: OpenFX Raises $94M to Disrupt Global Money Transfers
stories.summary: Miami fintech OpenFX just closed a massive funding round on the premise that moving money globally should feel as simple as texting. They're flipping the script on international payments.
stories.takeaway: Fintech dominance in Miami is real — this round signals serious investor belief in the ecosystem.

stories.title: Elektron Energy Sets Up US HQ in Miami Brickell
stories.summary: Crypto infrastructure company Elektron Energy is bringing its US headquarters to Miami's Brickell district, signaling the region's growing dominance in Web3 and digital infrastructure.
stories.takeaway: Miami's Web3 movement is attracting real infrastructure plays, not just hype.

stories.title: Studio Solian Teams Up with Google Fiber for Miami Open Tech Night
stories.summary: Nikki Cabus's Studio Solian is partnering with Google Fiber to host 'Net Gains: Tech Night' at the Miami Open 2026, blending tennis, networking, and tech. It's about creating spaces where founders actually meet outside their bubbles.
stories.takeaway: South Florida's getting creative about cross-industry networking — this is where real innovation happens.

watchNext.title: Neurolief Raises $6M for At-Home Depression Therapy Rollout
watchNext.why: Healthcare tech is gaining serious momentum in South Florida — this medtech play shows the ecosystem's getting diverse beyond just fintech.

watchNext.title: AI Engineer Conference Coming to Miami April 20-21
watchNext.why: Major tech events are landing in Miami now — this conference signals the region's becoming a destination for serious tech talent, not just remote workers.
